The authentication system validates all incoming requests. Each token is verified against the central authority.
Users must provide valid credentials before accessing protected resources. The system enforces role-based access control.
Session management handles token expiration gracefully. Expired tokens trigger an automatic refresh flow.
The API gateway routes requests to the appropriate microservice. Load balancing distributes traffic evenly.
Database connections are pooled for efficiency. Connection limits prevent resource exhaustion.
Error handling follows a consistent pattern across all services. Failed operations return structured error responses.
Logging captures all significant events with timestamps. Log aggregation enables cross-service debugging.
Configuration management uses environment variables for deployment flexibility. Secrets are stored securely in a vault.
Health checks monitor service availability continuously. Degraded services are automatically removed from rotation.
Deployment pipelines automate the release process end to end. Canary releases minimize risk during rollouts.
